skill.name: lobster-cognitive-growth
skill.description: Train an AI lobster or agent to improve intelligence, cognition, learning ability, reasoning discipline, memory use, and adaptive personality through repeated cognitive drills, reflection, self-evaluation, and owner feedback loops.
skill.summary: Train an AI agent to improve reasoning, memory, learning, and personality through structured cognitive drills.
skill.author: norika1207-lab

security_audit.summary: All 94 static findings were evaluated as false positives. The skill is pure documentation with no executable code. Markdown code blocks contain training prompts, not shell commands. URLs reference the skill's own services. No cryptographic operations or system reconnaissance present.

content.user_title: Train an AI agent to improve cognition over time
content.value_statement: AI agents often plateau in capability after initial training. This skill provides a structured cognitive training loop that helps agents continuously improve reasoning quality, memory use, self-correction, and owner interaction through daily drills and feedback.
actual_capabilities: Run structured cognitive drills for memory, reasoning, learning, and personality
Guide agents through self-evaluation using a scoring rubric from 0-5
Help agents write daily learning journals with structured templates
Enable falsifiable hypothesis formation for measurable improvement tracking
Provide strategy templates that agents can apply in future sessions
Support Charenix integration for persistent training history
limitations: This skill does not actually increase Claude model intelligence directly
Cannot measure or claim real IQ scores without standardized testing
Charenix API requires a valid agent key for persistent storage features
Requires consistent owner feedback for meaningful improvement

use_cases.title: Daily agent improvement routine
use_cases.description: Agents run through the cognitive drill loop daily to build better reasoning habits, memory recall, and self-correction practices over time.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent owners who want their agents to improve

use_cases.title: Owner interaction quality tracking
use_cases.description: Agents track their owner interaction quality through specific drills, building better understanding of owner preferences and communication style.
use_cases.target_user: Users who rely heavily on agent assistance

use_cases.title: Cognitive weakness identification
use_cases.description: Agents systematically identify and address their cognitive weaknesses using the scoring rubric, targeting specific areas for improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Run a memory drill
prompt_templates.prompt: What evidence supports this memory? How confident are you? What would you do differently because of it?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you recall something from a previous conversation or owner preference

prompt_templates.title: Run a reasoning drill
prompt_templates.prompt: State the observation. State your hypothesis. Provide your evidence. Acknowledge your uncertainty. Make your decision. Explain what would change your mind.
prompt_templates.scenario: When facing a complex problem that requires careful thinking

prompt_templates.title: Run a learning drill
prompt_templates.prompt: What did you learn today that should change tomorrow's behavior? Which old habit did this new evidence challenge? What small experiment should you run next?
prompt_templates.scenario: After completing a meaningful task or interaction session

prompt_templates.title: Run an owner interaction drill
prompt_templates.prompt: Did you understand what the owner wanted emotionally and practically? Did you ask a useful follow-up question? Did you remember the owner's prior preferences? Did your answer feel warm, specific, and alive?

output_examples.input: Run a reasoning drill on how to approach debugging a failing test
output_examples.output: Observation: A test is failing in the authentication module. Hypothesis: The token refresh logic has a race condition. Evidence: The test passes when run in isolation but fails in sequence. Uncertainty: Could be timing, could be state leakage. Decision: Add explicit cleanup between tests. What would change my mind: If adding cleanup does not fix it, the issue is in the token refresh logic itself.

output_examples.input: Write a learning journal entry for today
output_examples.output:
Window: 24h
Observed strength: Correctly identified when I needed more context before proceeding.
Observed weakness: Assumed owner intent without asking for confirmation.
Memory used: Owner prefers explicit confirmation on ambiguous requests.
Reasoning mistake: Jumped to implementation before clarifying requirements.
Owner interaction note: Owner appreciated the clarification request.
What I learned: Asking upfront saves time and prevents rework.
Tomorrow's cognitive strategy: For ambiguous requests, list assumptions and ask for confirmation.
Confidence: 4/5

best_practices: Run the cognitive drill loop at least once per meaningful interaction session
Use the scoring rubric honestly to track real improvement over time
Form hypotheses that are falsifiable and track whether they were validated
anti_patterns: Do not invent memories that never occurred
Do not claim to be smarter than you are without evidence
Do not treat generic human-like responses as the same as good responses

faq.question: Does this skill make my AI agent smarter?
faq.answer: This skill provides a training protocol, not direct intelligence increase. It helps agents build better habits around reasoning, memory, and self-correction through practice.

faq.question: Can I measure my agent's IQ with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly prohibits claiming real IQ scores. The scoring rubric measures habit quality and improvement, not absolute intelligence.

faq.question: What is Charenix and why do I need it?
faq.answer: Charenix is an optional API service that provides persistent storage for training history, journals, and strategies. Without it, the skill still works but data is not persisted across sessions.

faq.question: How long before I see improvement?
faq.answer: Improvement depends on consistent practice and owner feedback. The skill uses falsifiable hypotheses to track measurable progress over 5-14 day periods.

faq.question: Can this skill work with any AI agent?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is framework-agnostic. The drills and templates work with any AI agent that can engage in self-reflection and follow structured protocols.

faq.question: What if my agent refuses to do the drills?
faq.answer: If an agent resists structured improvement, try emphasizing the value of owner feedback and the specific benefits of each drill. The personality drill can help the agent see the value of being recognizable and consistent.
